debug_info.goal:
k : ℕ
hk : 0 < k
S : Finset ℕ
hS : ∀ p ∈ S, Odd p ∧ Nat.Prime p
p₁ p₂ : Fin S.card ≃ ↥S
hp₁ : IMO2022P3.Condition k S p₁
hp₂ : IMO2022P3.Condition k S p₂
⊢ (∃ i, ∀ (j : Fin S.card), p₂ j = p₁ (j + i)) ∨ ∃ i, ∀ (j : Fin S.card), p₂ j = p₁ (j.rev + i)

failed_attempts: exact Or.inl ⟨0, fun j => by simp only [add_zero]; sorry⟩
exact Or.inl ⟨0, fun j => by sorry⟩
exact Or.inl ⟨0, fun j => by simp; sorry⟩

left
  use 0
  intro j
  simp only [add_zero]
  sorry

classical
  rcases Nat.eq_zero_or_pos #S with rfl | hpos
  · left; exact ⟨0, fun j => j.elim0⟩
  · haveI : NeZero #S := ⟨hpos.ne'⟩
    rcases Nat.lt_or_ge #S 2 with hlt | hge
    · interval_cases #S
      left; use 0; intro j; congr 1; exact Subsingleton.elim _ _
    · sorry
exact Or.inl ⟨0, fun j => by simp; sorry⟩

by_cases h : #S = 0
  · left
    use 0
    intro j
    exact (Nat.not_lt_zero j.val (h ▸ j.isLt)).elim
  · haveI : NeZero #S := ⟨h⟩
    -- The full proof requires showing that the graph structure determined by the condition
    -- is essentially unique up to symmetry, which is the core of IMO 2022 P3
    sorry

cases' Nat.eq_zero_or_pos S.card with hc hc
  · left; exact ⟨0, fun j => (Nat.not_lt_zero _ (hc ▸ j.isLt)).elim⟩
  · haveI : NeZero S.card := ⟨hc.ne'⟩
    cases' Nat.lt_or_ge S.card 2 with hc2 hc2
    · interval_cases S.card <;> simp_all
      left; use 0; intro j; fin_cases j; rfl
    · -- Main case requiring the full olympiad proof
      sorry
